title: 10 best herbal teas for rheumatoid arthritis
intro_desc: Herbal teas for rheumatoid arthritis are infusions made from various plants and herbs that can help alleviate symptoms of this autoimmune disorder. These teas can reduce inflammation, manage pain, and promote overall well-being. Examples of herbal teas that help with rheumatoid arthritis include ginger, turmeric, green tea, and willow bark tea. Ginger tea, for instance, contains anti-inflammatory compounds that inhibit the production of inflammatory molecules, while turmeric tea is rich in curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Green tea, on the other hand, contains epigallocatechin-3-gallate (EGCG), which has been shown to reduce inflammation and slow down the progression of rheumatoid arthritis. Willow bark tea contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which can help manage pain and reduce inflammation. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals with rheumatoid arthritis can experience improved joint mobility, reduced pain and inflammation, and enhanced quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black alder teas helps with rheumatoid arthritis because it contains flavonoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint inflammation and pain. The tea also exhibits antioxidant activity, which helps to neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage in the joints. Additionally, black alder has been shown to modulate the immune response, reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines that exacerbate RA symptoms.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, in Black Alder tea have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce joint inflammation and alleviate symptoms of Rheumatoid Arthritis.

Tannins: The tannins present in Black Alder tea act as natural anti-inflammatory agents, helping to reduce the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and compounds that contribute to Joint pain and stiffness associated with RA.

Polyphenols: Polyphenolic compounds, such as gallic acid, in Black Alder tea have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to protect joints from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, a common phenomenon in Rheumatoid Arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
Bark: Contains salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin, which helps relieve pain and reduce swelling in the joints.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried black alder leaves alnus glutinosa in early spring for best flavor and aroma.
Measure one tablespoon of dried or two tablespoons of fresh black alder leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep the black alder leaves in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes depending on desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or cup to remove loose particles.
Let the tea cool down slightly before serving hot or chilled with sugar and lemon as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Celery teas helps with rheumatoid arthritis because it is rich in antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and phytochemicals that have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain. The apigenin and luteolin present in celery tea have potent anti-arthritic properties, which can help to decrease the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and prevent the progression of the disease. Additionally, celery tea's ability to improve blood circulation may also contribute to its beneficial effects on reducing symptoms associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Celery contains a unique combination of anti-inflammatory compounds, including apigenin and luteolin, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with rheumatoid arthritis.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in celery tea, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, may help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage in people with rheumatoid arthritis, promoting overall joint health.

Pain-relieving properties: Celery contains a natural pain reliever called 3-n-butylphthalide (3NB), which has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, potentially providing relief from chronic pain associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce joint pain and inflammation associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
Rhyzomes: Contain natural analgesics and anti-inflammatory agents that help to soothe joint pain and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh celery leaves and stalks or use dried celery powder for 2 cups of tea.
Combine 2 teaspoons of dried celery with 1 cup of boiling water in a large teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes allowing the flavors and nutrients to infuse and combine.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth removing any solids.
Serve the herbal celery tea hot or chilled with lemon slices and honey to taste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kinnikinnick teas helps with rheumatoid arthritis because it contains a unique blend of herbs, including willow bark, meadowsweet, and ginger, which work synergistically to reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain. The salicin present in willow bark mimics the effects of aspirin, while meadowsweet's methyl salicylate further enhances anti-inflammatory properties. Ginger's natural anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, also contribute to the tea's potent therapeutic effects, making it a natural and effective adjunct therapy for managing rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in joints, which can contribute to RA symptoms. Antioxidants like anthocyanins can neutralize free radicals that damage joint tissues.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Kinnikinnick teas, such as quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling, pain, and stiffness associated with RA. They can also inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Lignans: Lignans, a type of polyphenol, have been shown to possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of RA by reducing joint inflammation and improving immune function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of the kinnikinnick plant have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the kinnikinnick plant contain powerful anti-inflammatory agents that help to reduce joint swelling and ease stiffness caused by rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried kinnikinnick leaves per eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured kinnikinnick with boiling water in a teapot or infuser and allow it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot to remove loose leaves and solids.
Allow the tea to cool for a few minutes before serving or transferring it to an airtight container.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tarragon teas helps with rheumatoid arthritis because it contains a unique combination of bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work to reduce inflammation in the joints, thereby alleviating symptoms like pain and stiffness. Additionally, tarragon's ability to soothe digestive issues can help alleviate gastrointestinal discomfort commonly associated with rheumatoid arthritis, allowing for a better overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Tarragon tea's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development and progression of rheumatoid arthritis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The polyphenolic compounds in tarragon tea have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, which can help alleviate symptoms such as pain and swelling associated with rheumatoid arthritis.

Gastroprotective properties: Tarragon tea's gastroprotective effects may help reduce the risk of gastrointestinal complications, such as ulcers and digestive issues, that are common in people with rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis.
Rhyzomes: With their rich source of flavonoids and phenolic acids, rhyzomes exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making them a valuable addition to medicinal teas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tarragon leaves from your garden or purchase dried tarragon for this recipe using 2 tablespoons of loose-leaf herbs per quart.
Rinse the harvested tarragon leaves with cool water to remove any dirt or debris then pat them dry with a paper towel gently.
Combine the rinsed tarragon leaves and 1 quart of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser steeping for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a large cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth pressing on the solids to release all the flavorful liquid carefully.
Add honey to taste if desired and serve hot, allowing the flavors to meld together for at least 3 minutes before enjoying immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Indian frankincense teas helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its unique composition, which includes boswellic acids that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory compounds in the body. These acids have been shown to reduce joint swelling and pain by blocking the activity of enzymes involved in inflammation. Additionally, frankincense has anti-arthritic properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and promote cartilage health, making it a natural and effective way to alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Boswellic acids, particularly AKBA (3-acetyl-11-keto-beta-boswellic acid), exhibit potent anti-inflammatory activity, which helps reduce joint inflammation and pain associated with RA.
Pain Relief: The tea's boswellic acids also have analgesic properties, making it a natural pain reliever for patients suffering from Rheumatoid Arthritis' chronic joint pain.

Immunomodulatory Effects: Boswellia serrata extracts have been shown to modulate the immune system by reducing pro-inflammatory cytokines and increasing anti-inflammatory cytokines, which can help alleviate symptoms of RA.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
Bark: Contains boswellia alkaloids, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, making it effective in reducing joint inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Rinse one teaspoon of dried boswellia serrata bark or powder into a teapot.
Boil one cup of pure water in the teapot for three minutes and then remove from heat.
Steep the boswellia serrata in boiling water for seven to ten minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to eliminate any residue.
Serve the warm tea immediately, sweetened with honey if desired, and consume within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its high antioxidant content, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. The anti-inflammatory properties in white tea can help to soothe joint pain and stiffness, making it easier to move and perform daily activities. Additionally, the flavonoids present in white tea have been shown to reduce oxidative stress, which is often associated with rheumatoid arthritis. By incorporating white tea into one's diet, individuals may experience relief from symptoms and improved overall health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal white tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like catechins, flavonoids, and saponins which help reduce inflammation in the body, alleviating joint pain and swelling associated with rheumatoid arthritis.

Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content of herbal white tea helps neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress, reducing the damage caused by inflammation and improving overall joint health.

Cytokine-modulating activity: Some herbal white teas, like Turmeric-infused white tea, contain curcuminoids that modulate cytokine production, helping to reduce pro-inflammatory cytokines and promote anti-inflammatory ones, which can help alleviate rheumatoid arthritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids, roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis.
Bark: The bark of the white tea plant contains tannins and other compounds that have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and improve joint health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest camellia sinensis leaves at dawn when they are most fragrant and tender.
Wither the freshly harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area for several hours to remove excess moisture.
Roll the withered leaves into small balls or strips to release enzymes that start fermentation process.
Heat dried tea leaves in a pan or oven at 120°c for 10 minutes to stop fermentation and enhance flavor.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried white tea leaves in boiling water for 3-5 minutes then strain and serve hot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper teas helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's antioxidants, particularly capsaicin, work to reduce swelling and alleviate joint pain by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are chemicals that cause inflammation. Additionally, the tea's flavonoids help to improve blood circulation, reducing stiffness and promoting flexibility in affected joints, allowing for improved mobility and reduced discomfort associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_properties: Turmeric: Contains curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce joint pain and inflammation in people with rheumatoid arthritis.

Ginger: Has anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which may help reduce pain and swelling associated with rheumatoid arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules.

Devil's Claw (Harpagophytum procumbens): Contains harpagosides, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that may help reduce joint pain and stiffness in people with rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
Stems: The stems of the bell pepper plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe joint inflammation and relieve symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum peppers and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Chop the peppers into small pieces, yielding approximately 250 grams of material for the tea.
Combine the chopped peppers with one quart of boiling filtered water in a large glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 10 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together, before straining and discarding solids.
Store the cooled tea in an airtight container at room temperature or refrigerate it within 24 hours for optimal freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Safflower teas helps with rheumatoid arthritis because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as joint pain, stiffness, and swelling. The tea's polyunsaturated fatty acids, particularly omega-6 fatty acids, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which is a hallmark of RA. Additionally, safflower teas may also help improve joint mobility and reduce morning stiffness, making it a natural and effective complement to traditional RA treatments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Safflower tea has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis such as joint pain and swelling.

Pain-relieving properties: The tea's active compounds have been found to possess analgesic and anxiolytic (pain-reducing) effects, which can help alleviate joint pain and discomfort associated with rheumatoid arthritis.

Antioxidant properties: Safflower tea contains antioxidants that help protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to RA's progression and severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce joint pain and inflammation associated with rheumatoid arthritis.
Seeds: Containing linoleic acid, a fatty acid that has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and joint stiffness caused by rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried safflower flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Heat the water in a tea infuser or pot until it reaches a rolling boil.
Add the measured safflower flowers to the hot water and let steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving, and enjoy within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Shagbark hickory teas helps with rheumatoid arthritis because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids that have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work synergistically to reduce joint inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as pain, stiffness, and swelling associated with rheumatoid arthritis. By reducing oxidative stress and modulating the immune response, shagbark hickory teas may help mitigate disease progression and improve overall quality of life for individuals living with this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Shagbark Hickory contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes that have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, which are common symptoms of RA.
Antioxidant activity: The tea's antioxidant properties may help mitigate oxidative stress and cell damage caused by RA, potentially reducing joint damage and improving overall health.

Analgesic and sedative effects: The tea's analgesic properties may help relieve pain, while its sedative effects could promote relaxation and reduce inflammation-related discomfort, making it easier to manage RA symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and polyphenols, the roots of shagbark hickory have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and swelling associated with rheumatoid arthritis.

Bark: The bark of shagbark hickory contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making it effective in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh shagbark hickory leaves, selecting only those that are free from pests and diseases.
Rinse the harvested leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried shagbark hickory leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Add honey to taste and enjoy immediately, as the tea is best consumed freshly brewed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamon teas helps with rheumatoid arthritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the joints. The tea's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde and epicatechin, inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms associated with RA, including stiffness, joint pain, and swelling. Additionally, cinnamon's antioxidant properties may also help to protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, further contributing to its beneficial effects on RA sufferers.
remedy_properties: Cinnamon's anti-inflammatory compound cinnamaldehyde: Reduces joint inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating RA symptoms.

Antioxidant and free radical scavenging properties: Neutralizes oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals in the body, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue destruction characteristic of RA.

Cinnamon's ability to inhibit COX-2 enzyme production: Suppresses the production of cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2), an enzyme involved in pain processing and inflammation, providing relief from joint pain and stiffness associated with RA.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the cinnamon plant contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce joint pain and swelling associated with rheumatoid arthritis.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of the cinnamon plant are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried cinnamomum verum bark or two whole cinnamon sticks.
Combine the dried cinnamon with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the flavorful compounds and oils.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Serve hot, sweetening with honey if desired, and enjoy within 30 minutes.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat rheumatoid arthritis with herbal teas is by consuming regularly brewed teas known for their anti-inflammatory properties. Ginger, turmeric, green, and willow bark teas are some of the most effective options. These teas can help alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and improve joint mobility. For optimal results, aim to drink two to three cups of these herbal teas daily, and remember to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to rheumatoid arthritis that are treated with herbal teas include osteoarthritis and gout. For osteoarthritis, willow bark tea is often recommended due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Ginger tea is also used as it helps reduce inflammation and pain. For gout, teas made from ingredients like nettle, burdock root, and horsetail are often suggested because of their ability to cleanse the body of toxins and reduce uric acid levels. However, it's crucial to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any herbal treatment.
